urn:contentItem:6JHY-K3T3-RRNM-H4NX-00000-00SWINERTON INCORPORATED - RECENT INDUSTRY NEWS2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:55ZLexisNexisSTOCKSWINERTON INCORPORATED - RECENT INDUSTRY NEWS

DASHBOARD: SWINERTON INCORPORATED

PermID4296594671Websitehttp://www.swinerton.comIndustryConstruction & EngineeringAddress5707 SOUTHWEST PARKWAY, BLDG. 1, SUITE 200 | AUSTIN, TX 78735

ACTIVITIES:

Swinerton Incorporated provides commercial construction and construction management services in the Western United States. It offers general construction, modernization and renovation, management and consulting, preconstruction, corporate, and renewable energy services. The companys projects include aviation, cultural, education, federal, gaming and entertainment, healthcare, hospitality, interiors, mission critical, mixed-use/residential, office, renewable energy, retail, and special. Swinerton Incorporated was formerly known as Swinerton & Walberg Co. The company was founded in 1888 and is based in San Francisco, California with additional offices in California, Colorado, Hawaii, Texas, New Mexico, and Washington.

+ SWINERTON INCORPORATED [3 Press Releases]

+ SWINERTON TAPS PROVEN DUO TO DRIVE NATIONAL ACCOUNTS

With over $1 billion in growth and over 250 major projects under their belts, Swinerton's Chris Chany and Derek Jaschke step into new roles to drive client-focused expansion. Swinerton announces two strategic leadership promotions to continue delivering exceptional client experiences nationwide. Effective July 1, Chris Chany has been promoted to lead National Markets, while Derek Jaschke has been named Director of National Accounts, succeeding Chany.

Chany, known for his relationship-driven leadership style, previously served as Director of National Accounts, where he was instrumental in expanding Swinerton's client base and driving growth. Under his leadership, the national accounts sales model grew from $350 million to more than $1 billion in just four years. Chany has strengthened partnerships across multiple markets and sectors, generating repeat business.

"Chris' ability to strategize with teams from pre-sell to pursuit, through project execution and closeout, has allowed Swinerton to grow and solidify critical client relationships and seize growth opportunities for our teams," shares Don Adair, Swinerton's CRO. "Throughout his career, Chris has emphasized relationships, extraordinary client experiences, dependable project delivery and consistently generating repeat business."

Source: Company Website

+ SWINERTON INCORPORATED WILLIAM SILVA NAMED DIRECTOR OF NATIONAL MASS TIMBER

Instrumental leader to enhance Swinerton Builders' mass timber capabilities Swinerton, a national commercial general contractor and construction manager with office locations nationwide, continues its commitment to advancing and accelerating the adoption of mass timber construction with the appointment of William Silva as Director, National Mass Timber.

In this position, Silva will lead the creation of a Mass Timber Center of Excellence, a cross-functional initiative designed by Swinerton to drive innovation, collaboration and integrate the company's extensive general contracting expertise with its affiliate firm, Timberlab. Timberlab's specialized capabilities include mass timber procurement, manufacturing, fabrication, engineering and design. The center will serve as a hub for innovation, education, and operational excellence, empowering Swinerton teams nationwide to deliver exceptional mass timber projects and continue to be a trusted resource for its clients, design partners, and engineering partners.

Source: Company Website

+ SWINERTON TOPS-OUT LATEST MASS TIMBER MULTIFAMILY PROJECT IN CHARLOTTE

Swinerton, an industry-leading commercial general contractor with offices nationwide, announces the topping out of Cordo, an $80 million multifamily development in Charlotte's Optimist Park and the construction firm's fifth collaboration with Space Craft. Just blocks from The Joinery and Oxbow, also developed by Space Craft and built by Swinerton, Cordo is designed to be a walkable, transit-friendly community for active families and young professionals. The hybrid-structure broke ground last fall and is expected to be ready for occupancy by summer 2027.

"There is a distinct comfort level when you have a long-term relationship with your construction crew that spans multiple projects," says Brian Nicholson, director of construction, Space Craft. "Cordo is the fifth project we've embarked on with Swinerton, and the team continues to impress us with an unwavering commitment to quality and ability to deliver on our vision. With each community, we set out to build a sustainable, modern neighborhood for today. Swinerton has been instrumental in helping us shape the Mill District into one of Charlotte's hottest neighborhoods."

Situated on a three-acre site in Charlotte's Optimist Park community, Cordo totals seven floors with 3,000 square feet of street-level retail just steps from Cordelia Park, Optimist Hall and LYNX Blue Line light rail access. The 325,000-square foot development features 287studio, one-, two-, three- and four-bedroom units and offers premium amenities including a rooftop terrace, private courtyard, electric bicycles for rent, a fitness center, a shared dining area and coworking and meeting space. A Mass timber brings a biophilic element into each residence with cross-laminated timber (CLT) decks creating exposed timber ceilings. Charlotte architecture firm Shook Kelley designed the community located at 515 Parkwood Ave.

Source: Company Website

+ AUSTIN INDUSTRIES, INC. [3 Press Releases]

+ BARTON MALOW COMPANY [4 Press Releases]

+ BARTON MALOW + SHERIDAN CONSTRUCTION SET TO BUILD NEW MACON ARENA

MACON, GEORGIA - Barton Malow, in association with Sheridan Construction, has been chosen as the Construction Manager for the new Macon Arena, a major project that will replace the current Macon Coliseum and serve as a key part of the region's expanding entertainment opportunities.

Located southeast of Atlanta in Macon-Bibb County, the planned facility is designed to host various multipurpose events like minor league hockey, basketball games, high school tournaments, concerts, conferences, trade shows, and more. The new arena will offer a top-tier guest experience, featuring premium seating options like luxury suites, club suites, and loge boxes, as well as modern fan amenities such as clubs, concessions, restrooms, locker rooms, and integrated technologies. Back-of-house and support spaces will ensure the venue functions as a state-of-the-art civic facility, offering performers, event planners, and organizations a great experience.

"This arena won't be just a venue, it will be a destination and major economic engine that creates opportunities, attracts visitors, and keeps our city competitive for years to come," said Macon-Bibb Mayor Lester Miller.

As Macon's entertainment landscape continues to expand with the new Atrium Health Amphitheater and the recently renovated Macon City Auditorium, the new arena is poised to further elevate the region's growing lineup of elite venues.

"We're thrilled to partner with Sheridan Construction and bring Barton Malow's expertise to Macon-Bibb to deliver a top-tier arena. The new venue is set to become a central location in Georgia for sports, entertainment, and community gatherings," said Barton Malow Office Director Blake Boyd. "This project is an investment in Macon-Bibb County's future, and we're excited to support the local community every step of the way."

Adds Sheridan Construction President + CEO Christy Kovac. "It's a privilege for our firm to return to Macon's legacy of entertainment and civic pride, having built the original coliseum 57 years ago. Partnering with Barton Malow on this transformative new arena is both an honor and a responsibility. We believe this project will serve as a powerful economic and tourism engine, attracting visitors, creating opportunities, and enriching the lives of everyone in our community for generations to come."

Construction is planned to commence in summer 2026, with the project anticipated to reach completion in 2028.

Source: Company Website

+ BARTON MALOW COMPANY LUKE KNOCHEL PROMOTED TO VICE PRESIDENT OF PROJECT CONTROLS

Luke's project controls leadership role spans multiple geographic regions and consists of overseeing directors and managers across Barton Malow's operations.

Barton Malow has announced the promotion of Luke Knochel to Vice President of Project Controls.

In his new role, Luke leads a multi‑disciplinary team responsible for core functions such as cost and schedule management, forecasting, project start‑up, accounts receivable and payable processes, and change management. His leadership spans multiple geographic regions, overseeing directors and managers across Barton Malow's operations.

Luke started at Barton Malow in 2013 as a Cost Analyst and has served in various project controls and finance leadership roles throughout his tenure. He oversaw accounts payable and accounts receivable, change management, forecasting, reporting, and more on the Little Caesars Arena project, then went on to supervise Barton Malow's offices in Chicago, Columbus, and Nashville. After a promotion to Project Controls Manager overseeing the then-Midwest business unit, Luke transitioned into a corporate finance role managing the shared service department and implementing new accounts payable software.

+ BARTON MALOW COMPANY BUILDING FOR THE BRAND: CONSTRUCTING COLLEGE ATHLETIC FACILITIES IN THE NIL ERA

NIL athletic construction: Many modern facilities now include dedicated content creation zones where athletes can produce high-quality photos and videos for social media and sponsorship campaigns.

On July 1, 2021, the NCAA formally adopted a policy allowing college athletes to earn money for their name, image, and likeness (NIL).

At the time, one of my acquaintances, Matt Wilson, was serving as the Senior Director of Development for Athletics at Embry-Riddle Aeronautical University. He witnessed firsthand the immediate impact that NIL had on college athletics.

Today, Matt sees things from the other side of the table. After leaving Embry-Riddle in 2022, he spent two years as Executive Director of NIL Collective Operations + Development at Athlete Forward Initiative. Now he runs his own consulting group designed to provide leadership, fundraising, communication, and culture development training for college athletic departments.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [4 Press Releases]

+ TURNER MARKS 124TH ANNIVERSARY WITH FOUNDERS' MONTH OF COMMUNITY IMPACT

In celebration of its founding in May 1902, Turner Construction Company is proud to launch its annual Founders' Month, a global effort that brings together more than 16,000 employees in service to the communities where they live and work.

The construction industry hired approximately four million workers in the United States but added only approximately 400,000 jobs in 2025. The Associated Builders and Contractors estimates the industry will need to attract roughly 365,000 net additional workers in 2026 to keep pace with demand, with skilled trades availability emerging as one of the most critical constraints on project delivery.

With that challenge in mind, this year's Founders' Month places an emphasis on strengthening the construction workforce pipeline - connecting community engagement with training, access, and career opportunities that help bring more people into the trades and support them in building long-term careers.

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION ENTERPRISES, INC. [2 Press Releases]

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION BREAKS GROUND ON $190 MILLION CONTRACT FOR STATE-OF-THE-ART DRINKING WATER FACILITY

DENVER, Colo. (Sept. 25, 2025) - PCL Construction, Inc. celebrated the groundbreaking of a $190 million contract to build the Westminster Boulevard Drinking Water Facility - an innovative project that employs advanced treatment technology to ensure clean water to residents.

The City of Westminster entrusted the PCL team to build the project's most critical components. As the lead contractor, PCL will manage the construction of cutting-edge filtration and treatment technologies. The facility will feature advanced oxidation and filtration systems designed to improve taste and odor, while enhancing resiliency against fluctuations in incoming water quality.

Designed with long-term sustainability in mind, the project replaces aging infrastructure and will provide reliable drinking water to nearly 33,000 homes and businesses. Once operational the facility will have the capacity to treat 14.7 million gallons of water per day (MGD).

"PCL is proud to celebrate the opening of the Westminster Boulevard Drinking Water Facility with the City of Westminster. This is an essential project that secures safe, reliable drinking water for the community," said Ankur Talwar, PCL's West region district manager.

The groundbreaking ceremony brought together Westminster City Council members, build partners, local leaders, and community members to celebrate the future site.

The facility is slated to be finished in 2028.

Source: Company Website

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION AND PHOENIX CHILDREN'S: BUILDING A STRONGER FUTURE FOR PEDIATRIC CARE

PHOENIX, Ariz. (November 10, 2025) - PCL Construction, Inc. and Phoenix Children's are proud to celebrate a partnership dedicated to advancing pediatric health and supporting children facing critical medical challenges in Arizona.

For eight consecutive years, PCL Construction in Arizona has deepened its commitment to Phoenix Children's, increasing its annual donation to $50,000 in support of pediatric cancer care. This ongoing collaboration directly benefits children by funding medical specialists, cutting-edge technology, and family-centered care.

"Our partnership with Phoenix Children's is about more than sponsorship-it's about building hope and supporting families during their most difficult times," said Josh Yates, construction risk manager at PCL Construction. "We are honored to contribute to the hospital's mission and to help create brighter futures for children in our community."

As part of this partnership, PCL proudly sponsors Phoenix Children's annual Big Dig for Kids event. This unique experience gives pediatric patients a chance to explore construction equipment, participate in hands-on activities, and enjoy a day filled with fun and inspiration. The event not only raises vital funds for pediatric cancer care but also brings moments of joy to children and families, helping them envision a future beyond illness.

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. RYAN HOUSE DEBUTS TEEN SUITES FOR KIDS LIVING LONGER WITH LIFE-LIMITING CONDITIONS

Ryan House, a nonprofit that provides respite and end-of-life care to children with life-limiting conditions, recently capped off its 15th anniversary year by unveiling two newly renovated teen suites. The renovations allow Ryan House to better serve children who are now surviving well into adolescence and young adulthood and were made possible through generous support from McCarthy Building Companies, which selected Ryan House as a recipient of the company's Heart Hats(TradeMark) grants, and ARC Document Solutions.

Thanks to advances in diagnostics, disease management, and comprehensive care, many children with life-limiting conditions are now living longer than once expected. Ryan House is evolving alongside the children and families it serves, making meaningful changes to better support teens through environments designed specifically for this stage of life.

The renovation was spearheaded by McCarthy's Specialized Solutions Group, which focuses on complex renovations and technical builds within healthcare and other specialized environments. The donation involved the renovation of two private suites specifically designed for teenagers and features superhero-themed decor provided by ARC that fully immerses them in a comic book-inspired atmosphere. The rooms also now include fully adjustable desks to accommodate different mobility needs, allowing teens to comfortably do schoolwork or creative activities. Each suite is equipped with the latest state-of-the-art gaming system and adaptive controllers, plus customizable LED lighting that can change colors based on each teen's preferences. Additionally, bathroom updates were made to allow for enhanced comfort and accessibility.

"This renovation speaks to how pediatric care has changed over the past decade and a half," said Dr. Wendy Bernatavicius, medical director at Ryan House and division chief of primary, complex care and adolescent medicine at Phoenix Children's Hospital. "We're seeing more adolescents and young adults with complex needs living vibrant lives. Improved medical care, early intervention and whole-family support have extended the lifespan of many of these teens. That means rethinking the environments where they're cared for. These teen suites are not just rooms. They're an affirmation of identity, belonging, and respect for each young person who calls Ryan House their home away from home."

During their recent holiday break, Ryan House welcomed two familiar guests, brothers Mason and Jaxton, who are living with Duchenne muscular dystrophy, as the first teens to stay in the newly renovated suites. While they've stayed at Ryan House before, this visit marked the first time they experienced the upgraded rooms designed specifically with teens, like them, in mind.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC GREEN FROM THE INSIDE OUT: BERKELEY LAB SETS NEW STANDARDS

Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory's Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center sets new sustainability standards. Berkeley Lab Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center (BioEPIC) unites scientists from LBNL's biosciences and earth and environmental sciences areas in a four-story, 71,000-square-foot facility that sets a new standard for sustainability. Completed in March, the facility offers a suite of next-generation tools designed to study how microbial communities interact with plants and soil to influence the environment.

Inside, 200 researchers perform carefully controlled experiments on microbe-soil-plant interactions with instruments and computing infrastructure that connect BioEPIC to its field research sites. These unique climate research environments, along with three additional levels of labs and collaborative spaces, are housed in an all-electric facility designed to maximize energy efficiency and resiliency.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC FROM CONCEPT TO REALITY: HARNESSING 3D PRINTING AT CLARK'S MID-ATLANTIC YARD

Clark Construction's Mid-Atlantic Yard in Upper Marlboro, Maryland, is a powerhouse of specialized services, from fleet oversight to custom fabrication. This diverse facility is also part of a transformative initiative: the integration of 3D printing for prototyping innovative custom solutions to solve complex challenges.

The method came to life when faced with a challenge: designing a custom cleaning piece for a drill rig auger. While the traditional route would involve costly and time-consuming steel fabrication for a prototype, Charles Harman III, a craft and machine shop supervisor, and his team explored a new solution for this type of fabrication and successfully used a 3D printer to create a scaled model. The successful prototype demonstrated the efficacy of the system, and once the 3D printed model was approved, the team began assembling the steel-fabricated version.

value.Title: HENSEL PHELPS CONSTRUCTION CO., INC. - RECENT PEER AND INDUSTRY INSIGHTS

urn:contentItem:6JJ0-2TV3-S0S7-12V3-00000-00HENSEL PHELPS CONSTRUCTION CO., INC. - RECENT PEER AND INDUSTRY INSIGHTS2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:39:01ZLexisNexisSTOCKHENSEL PHELPS CONSTRUCTION CO., INC. - RECENT PEER AND INDUSTRY INSIGHTS

DASHBOARD: HENSEL PHELPS CONSTRUCTION CO

PermID4296577863Websitehttp://www.henselphelps.comIndustryConstruction

ACTIVITIES:

Hensel Phelps Construction Co., Inc., founded in 1937, is an employee-owned general contractor headquartered in Greeley, Colorado. With over 4,000 employees across 10 U.S. offices, it delivers construction and renovation projects in aviation, commercial, healthcare, education, and government sectors. Generating over $6 billion annually, notable projects include the Pentagon renovation and San Jose International Airport modernization. In 2024, Hensel Phelps advanced its use of virtual design and construction (VDC) technologies, with VDC Manager Mark Blacklin winning a drone challenge for high-quality imaging. Its culture emphasizes safety, collaboration, and innovation, fostering a commitment to excellence through employee ownership. Competing with Turner Construction, Hensel Phelps stands out for its integrated project delivery and sustainable practices. Recent initiatives include expanding renewable energy projects and enhancing safety programs like Construction Safety Week.

+ HENSEL PHELPS CONSTRUCTION CO [3 News That Matters]

+ TRACK3D AND HENSEL PHELPS ACCELERATE AI-DRIVEN ANALYTICS FOR LARGE-SCALE CONSTRUCTION

Track3D and Hensel Phelps announced a multi-year agreement to extend Track3D's Reality Intelligence across more than 200 active projects, moving beyond a pilot phase as of Apr. 1, 2026.The partnership aims to standardize portfolio-wide progress monitoring, enabling earlier detection of on-site issues and reducing delays on complex builds while boosting schedule reliability and oversight.A San Francisco International Airport case study demonstrated the platform's proactive decision-making through 360-degree site walks and involvement of field personnel, reinforcing its value in infrastructure work; a March update introduced an admin dashboard, Groups capability and capture-free floorplan status capture to streamline management.

+ HENSEL PHELPS ELEVATES MICAH CHOY TO LEAD SUPERINTENDENT ON REGIONAL KITCHEN PROJECT

Micah (Pono) Choy has been promoted to project superintendent at Hensel Phelps, overseeing the Hawaii Department of Education Regional Kitchen – North Central Oahu project in Wahiawa. As of 1st April 2026, Choy's promotion marks a milestone in his career with the firm, which he joined in 2018.In his tenure with Hensel Phelps, Choy has worked on notable projects in Hawaii and Guam across aviation, wastewater, and federal markets.His promotion underscores Hensel Phelps' focus on cultivating talent and positioning Choy to lead the Regional Kitchen initiative, which aims to enhance educational facilities in Hawaii.

+ TRACK3D AND HENSEL PHELPS SIGNAL SCALABLE AI IN CONSTRUCTION WITH 200-PROJECT DEPLOYMENT

As of 25th March 2026, Track3D, an AI-driven construction technology company, announced a multi-year enterprise agreement with Hensel Phelps that expands from a pilot to deployment on more than 200 active projects.Hensel Phelps plans to standardize Track3D’s Reality Intelligence platform across its portfolio for progress monitoring, signaling confidence in the technology’s scalability across diverse project types. The rollout is expected to deepen recurring revenue, strengthen customer loyalty, and create a high-profile reference account that could attract additional large contractor deals.In a notable case, Track3D highlighted a San Francisco International Airport deployment where Hensel Phelps used the platform to shift from reactive to proactive decision-making, improving progress tracking and coordination across trades without major workflow changes. The company also detailed a March product release that adds an admin dashboard and Groups for centralized portfolio visibility, plus capture-free progress tracking on floorplans for easier site documentation.Track3D is pursuing its go-to-market strategy with a focus on specialty contractors and plans to participate in the AWCI Convention & Expo 2026, signaling momentum in its growth as it shifts toward automated production tracking and enhanced margins.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ STRATEGIC JV SECURES MAJOR O&M CENTER FOR AUSTIN LIGHT RAIL AS PART OF $7.1B PROJECT

This announcement was made on 28th April 2026. A joint venture of Kiewit Building Group and Austin Commercial has moved forward with a design-build contract to construct an operations and maintenance facility for the US$7.1 billion Austin Light Rail line. The project will support a 10-mile corridor with 15 stations and all-electric trains planned to operate every five to ten minutes.The O&M facility will store, service, and dispatch trains and provide workspace for operators, maintenance crews, and support staff.Ten design-phase subcontractors are involved, and the overall effort is expected to generate thousands of jobs during both the design and construction phases. ATP Chief Executive Greg Canally noted the project as a major step forward and highlighted the value of a locally experienced partner like Kiewit.Earlier, Aecom-led LINC Austin was named the delivery partner for the first phase, with a roster that includes Parsons, STV, Turner & Townsend, Ardmore Roderick, and CAS Consulting.

+ KAP JV CHOSEN TO LEAD LIGHT RAIL O&M FACILITY AS PROJECT CONNECT ADVANCES

The Austin Transit Partnership revealed that Kiewit Austin Partnership (KAP), a Kiewit Building Group Inc. and Austin Commercial joint venture, will design and build the Operations and Maintenance Facility, accelerating pre-construction activities. The development was announced on 21st April 2026.The 62-acre OMF site will sit on the southwest edge of the Montopolis neighborhood, near U.S. 183 and SH 71, and will store and service up to 40 light rail vehicles. Property acquisitions tied to the project will affect dozens of existing businesses, with Airport Commerce Park highlighted as an area of relocation concern.KAP’s design team already includes about 10 subcontractors, with expectations for expanded involvement as construction ramps up. The broader Project Connect program is expected to generate substantial job activity across Texas during design and construction, while the OMF itself is projected to support around 300 long-term jobs once in service. Mainline construction could begin in 2027, with service anticipated for the early 2030s, following the Federal Transit Administration's January 2026 EIS/ROD clearance for final design and land acquisition.

+ STRATEGIC PARTNERSHIP ADVANCES U.S. CLEAN HYDROGEN AND HEAVY WATER PROJECT TO PRE-FEED

Aternium has selected Kiewit Engineering Group Inc. to conduct the pre-front-end engineering and design for its first integrated hydrogen and heavy water facility, aligning with the company's plan to deploy a $1 billion program to produce these materials domestically. The announcement was made on 26th March 2026.The Mid-Atlantic site will address demand for low-carbon energy and bolster U.S. capabilities in heavy water, which is currently sourced largely from foreign suppliers. Andrew Cottone noted that the collaboration with Kiewit reflects a shared commitment to technical excellence and safety.The project combines clean hydrogen production with a patent-pending heavy water isolation process, aiming to reduce hydrogen costs and strengthen domestic supply. The pre-FEED will set the scope and design foundations for future development, positioning the venture at the crossroads of energy transition and advanced manufacturing.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ ALIGNED IAD-06 DATA CENTER REACHES STRUCTURAL MILESTONE AS TURNER MOVES TOWARD FIT-OUT

Turner Construction Company celebrated a major milestone at the Aligned IAD-06 Data Center in Frederick, Maryland, with the completion of the structure for the 72-megawatt facility on the 28th of April 2026. The project has logged more than 80,000 craft hours and involves about 345 skilled workers. The center spans 450,000 square feet on a 75-acre campus and features a two-story structural steel frame, an insulated precast exterior, and a high-efficiency hybrid cooling system that blends liquid and air-cooled technologies. "Topping out this facility represents more than completing the structural frame," commented Nick Sevier, Operations Manager at Turner. "It reflects the strength of our partnership with Aligned, the dedication of our trade partners, and Turner’s commitment to delivering this complex, high-performance data center safely and efficiently." With the structure now in place, attention turns to interior fit-out and readiness as the project progresses toward activation and continued support for the region’s digital infrastructure.

+ TURNER LEADS 11-STORY GEISINGER TOWER BUILD IN $900M VALLEY UPGRADE

Turner Construction has kicked off vertical construction on the $900 million Geisinger Wyoming Valley Medical Center expansion in Wilkes-Barre, a milestone in one of the region's largest health care investments. Steel is rising on an 11-story, 600,000-square-foot tower that will increase beds from 356 to 500 and add operating rooms from 12 to 18, while enlarging the emergency department and ensuring all private rooms. The project targets a first-quarter 2028 opening. This announcement was issued on 28th April 2026.David Kaminski, Turner’s Vice President and Senior General Manager, said the project represents expanded access and a modern health care platform for families across northeastern Pennsylvania, noting the company’s pride in partnering with Geisinger. The expansion is expected to support up to 3,800 construction jobs and generate about $1 billion in local economic impact. Peak onsite staffing could reach 450 workers, with Geisinger planning to hire 400 more after completion.Originally opened in 1981, the Wyoming Valley campus has grown to meet demand, and the new tower will bring capabilities on par with major academic medical centers serving large cities.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION TO SPEARHEAD WESTCOURT ORLANDO CONSTRUCTION IN MAJOR DOWNTOWN MIXED-USE SCHEME

Turner Construction has been named the general contractor for the Westcourt Orlando mixed-use project, a development planned for West Church Street in downtown Orlando. The announcement was made by developers on the 22nd of April, 2026, marking a milestone in the project’s progression. The decision to partner with Turner stems from a collaborative approach taken by JMA Ventures. Todd Chapman, president of JMA Ventures, said that Turner’s team demonstrated exceptional capabilities in prior complex builds, including coordinating infrastructure for rocket launches on Florida’s Space Coast. He recalled an early meeting in which he told the team, "If you guys can send things into space, I’m pretty sure you can work with us." Turner has been involved in the Westcourt project since 2022, handling pre-construction with JMA Ventures and Machete Group. Early site activities such as utility mapping and surveying are underway to ensure a smooth transition into the next phases. The Westcourt project is valued at approximately $500 million and will include a hotel, residential units, office space, retail, and a live entertainment venue adjacent to the Kia Center, with peak on-site employment around 800 workers.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS [3 News That Matters]

+ STRATEGIC MERGER UNITES AUDDIA AND THRAMANN AS MCCARTHY FINNEY, INC.

Both boards have unanimously approved the merger, with Auddia's independent special committee affirming the deal's fairness and supported by a fairness opinion from Houlihan Capital. Governance continuity is planned as Auddia's four directors will join the McCarthy Finney board, while Jeff Thramann and John Mahoney will continue as CEO and CFO. Thramann will also be subject to a 180-day lock-up on his McCarthy Finney shares. The deal is expected to close in the second quarter of 2026, subject to Auddia shareholder approval, SEC effectiveness of a Form S-4 filing, and Nasdaq listing requirements. McCarthy Finney aims to pursue AI applications across distributed AI data centers, healthtech value-based care, and AI-driven travel services, leveraging Auddia's technology portfolio.

+ MCCARTHY BUILDS MOMENTUM IN HEALTHCARE CONSTRUCTION WITH CALIFORNIA TOWER MILESTONE

The California Tower milestone aligns with UC Davis Health's broader Vision 2030, a plan to more than double the campus footprint. Opening is targeted for 2030, finalizing a major expansion that will deliver increased patient capacity and upgraded facilities. In addition to this project, McCarthy recently completed the Plaza West Tower at Barnes-Jewish Hospital in St. Louis in December, underscoring the firm's active role in healthcare construction even as early 2026 market indicators hint at a potential slowdown in some healthcare-project planning.

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS SETS INDUSTRY BENCHMARK WITH 2024 GHG EMISSIONS DISCLOSURE

McCarthy Holdings has released its updated 2024 GHG Emissions Report, issued on 16th February 2026 and developed in collaboration with Gravity. The report maps Scope 1, Scope 2, and key Scope 3 emissions, with the Scope 1 and 2 data independently audited and given limited assurance, demonstrating a commitment to transparency in the construction sector.As an employee-owned firm, McCarthy emphasizes its core values and regulatory readiness, with sustainability anchored in three priorities: fully understanding environmental impact, acting responsibly toward people and communities, and efficiently managing energy use. The company notes that its emissions data inform broader practices, including biodiversity work and responsible fuel management, while its digital tools and AI support smarter project decisions.In addition, McCarthy highlights an advanced PV recycling program for solar panels, partnering with SEIA-approved recyclers to divert waste from landfills and promote a circular economy. Through data-driven insights, the firm aims to strengthen communities and set a high standard for responsible construction.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ CARLY REINER CACERES TAKES HELM OF HR FOR NATIONAL CAMPUS RECRUITING AT CLARK CONSTRUCTION

Carly Reiner Caceres has assumed the role of Director of Human Resources – National Campus Recruiting at Clark Construction Group, effective Apr. 29, 2026, tasked with coordinating nationwide campus initiatives to expand early-career talent pipelines for the company.Her career blends leadership in People Operations at Proper, where she guided HR strategy in a hybrid environment, with roles at MILLER as Director of Internal Operations and nearly a decade at Accenture as Talent Strategy Manager and Campus Recruiting Lead.Caceres’ experience spans full-cycle recruiting, stakeholder engagement, and employer branding, capabilities Clark Construction aims to leverage to attract top talent from universities across the United States. She has also led campus recruiting efforts at Grant Thornton LLP, strengthening internship programs and student engagement.Clark Construction, founded in 1906, emphasizes community impact and project excellence. Caceres’ background in communications and external relations from early roles at Charleston Magazine and the Hirshhorn Museum is expected to support her work in nurturing the next generation of construction professionals.

+ CLARK CONTRACTS TO DELIVER REPAIRS, MAINTENANCE AND REFURBISHMENT UNDER RENFREWSHIRE COUNCIL FRAMEWORK

Clark Contracts has been named to Renfrewshire Council's multi-trades framework to improve repair, maintenance and refurbishment services for domestic properties in the area. The appointment was announced on 11th February 2026 and runs through 2027.The framework covers a broad range of multi-trade works, enabling the council to better manage both responsive and planned maintenance across its housing stock and to deliver more timely service to residents.Clark Contracts is already active in the public sector, with commitments including Scotland Excel’s New Build Residential Framework and NHS National Services Scotland’s Minor Works Framework, and involvement with Crown Commercial Service and Scottish Procurement Alliance projects.Gordon Cunningham, managing director, noted the company’s track record of more than £200 million in refurbishment projects over three years and said the appointment strengthens the services available to Renfrewshire residents.

+ THE VILLAGES SET FOR ₱4-B MIXED-USE PROJECT AS CDC ENDORSES 30-MONTH BUILD

Clark Development Corp. has approved an amendment to PKR Corp.'s lease for a ₱4-billion mixed-use hub at The Villages, a 67-hectare master-planned sports and lifestyle destination in Clark Freeport. The amendment grants PKR a 30-month timeframe to construct the project and introduces performance safeguards to help ensure on-schedule delivery. CDC president and CEO Atty. Agnes VST Devanadera described the move as reinforcing investor confidence and the community’s integration within The Villages, while PKR Corp. president Daekwon Jung expressed gratitude for the partnership and pledged timely completion. PKR operates under Kyeryong Construction Industrial Co., Ltd., a South Korean builder with recognized strengths in safety, technology, and workplace practices. The development aligns with CDC’s strategy to accelerate investments, foster well-planned communities, and strengthen Clark as a business-friendly destination that attracts both local and international capital.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JHN-7S43-SK5D-33C4-00000-00HENSEL PHELPS CONSTRUCTION CO., INC. - COMPREHENSIVE NEWS FROM INDUSTRY AND ITS PEERS2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:39:01ZLexisNexisSTOCKHENSEL PHELPS CONSTRUCTION CO., INC. - COMPREHENSIVE NEWS FROM INDUSTRY AND ITS PEERS

DASHBOARD: HENSEL PHELPS CONSTRUCTION CO

PermID4296577863Websitehttp://www.henselphelps.comIndustryConstruction

ACTIVITIES:

Hensel Phelps Construction Co., Inc., founded in 1937, is an employee-owned general contractor headquartered in Greeley, Colorado. With over 4,000 employees across 10 U.S. offices, it delivers construction and renovation projects in aviation, commercial, healthcare, education, and government sectors. Generating over $6 billion annually, notable projects include the Pentagon renovation and San Jose International Airport modernization. In 2024, Hensel Phelps advanced its use of virtual design and construction (VDC) technologies, with VDC Manager Mark Blacklin winning a drone challenge for high-quality imaging. Its culture emphasizes safety, collaboration, and innovation, fostering a commitment to excellence through employee ownership. Competing with Turner Construction, Hensel Phelps stands out for its integrated project delivery and sustainable practices. Recent initiatives include expanding renewable energy projects and enhancing safety programs like Construction Safety Week.

+ HENSEL PHELPS CONSTRUCTION CO [3 News That Matters]

+ HENSEL PHELPS SIGNS MULTI-YEAR DEAL WITH TRACK3D TO STANDARDIZE CONSTRUCTION PROGRESS MONITORING ACROSS PORTFOLIO

Track3D and Hensel Phelps have signed a multi-year agreement to roll out Track3D's Reality Intelligence platform across more than 200 active projects, elevating a pilot into a portfolio-wide deployment as of Apr. 1, 2026. The deal underscores Hensel Phelps' commitment to leveraging AI-driven construction data across its operations.The arrangement envisions standardizing Track3D for portfolio-wide progress monitoring, enabling earlier identification of on-site issues and reducing delays on complex projects, while improving schedule reliability and accountability across diverse sites.A San Francisco International Airport case study showcased the platform's ability to move teams from reactive problem-solving to proactive decision-making through 360-degree site walks and active involvement by existing field personnel, minimizing workflow disruption.In its March update, Track3D introduced an admin dashboard, Groups functionality and capture-free progress tracking directly on floorplans, designed to give major contractors centralized visibility and easier user management.The company is expanding its market approach by targeting specialty contractors, with plans to participate in the AWCI Convention & Expo 2026 to demonstrate automation of production tracking and reduction of manual reporting for drywall and ceiling professionals. Taken together, the developments reinforce Track3D's competitive position and lay groundwork for future growth.

+ MICAH (PONO) CHOY NAMED PROJECT SUPERINTENDENT FOR HAWAII REGIONAL KITCHEN AT HENSEL PHELPS

Micah (Pono) Choy has been promoted to project superintendent at Hensel Phelps, overseeing the Hawaii Department of Education Regional Kitchen – North Central Oahu project in Wahiawa. As of 1st April 2026, the promotion marks a milestone in his career with the firm, which he joined in 2018.Choy's experience spans aviation, wastewater, and federal market projects in Hawaii and Guam, where he has helped deliver complex assignments from planning to execution.Choy's collaborative leadership has allowed him to build strong partnerships across teams and consistently drive project outcomes. Hensel Phelps regards this promotion as part of its ongoing effort to develop talent from within; the Regional Kitchen project is essential for upgrading Hawaii's educational facilities and will benefit from his leadership.

+ HENSEL PHELPS ADOPTS TRACK3D REALITY INTELLIGENCE FOR PORTFOLIO-WIDE PROGRESS MONITORING

As of 25th March 2026, Track3D, an AI-driven construction technology company, announced a multi-year enterprise agreement with Hensel Phelps that expands from a pilot to deployment on more than 200 active projects.Hensel Phelps plans to standardize Track3D’s Reality Intelligence platform across its portfolio for progress monitoring, signaling confidence in the technology’s scalability across diverse project types. The extensive rollout is expected to deepen recurring revenue, strengthen customer loyalty, and create a high-profile reference account that could attract additional large contractor deals.In a notable application, Track3D pointed to a San Francisco International Airport case study where Hensel Phelps used the platform to shift from reactive to proactive decision-making, improving progress tracking and coordination across trades without major workflow changes.On the development front, Track3D disclosed a March release to boost enterprise oversight and workflow efficiency, including an admin dashboard and Groups functionality to centralize portfolio visibility and streamline user management, plus capture-free progress tracking on floorplans, enhancing operational flexibility for enterprise users.The company is also sharpening its go-to-market by targeting specialty contractors and will participate in the AWCI Convention & Expo 2026, signaling momentum in the industry as it aims to automate production tracking and protect margins. Together with the Hensel Phelps deal and the SFO case study, the March upgrades mark meaningful commercial traction for Track3D.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ KIEWIT-AUSTIN COMMERCIAL JV TO DELIVER OPERATIONS & MAINTENANCE FACILITY FOR AUSTIN LIGHT RAIL

On 28th April 2026, a joint venture between Kiewit Building Group and Austin Commercial secured a design-build contract to construct an operations and maintenance facility for the US$7.1 billion Austin Light Rail line, which will span 15 stations along a 10-mile corridor and feature all-electric trains expected to run every five to ten minutes through much of the day.Kiewit and Austin Commercial will design and build the facility where Austin’s light rail vehicles will be stored, serviced, and dispatched to ensure reliable daily service.In addition to storage and maintenance, the facility will provide workspace for operators, maintenance crews, and support staff, contributing to the overall functionality of the system.The project includes about 10 design-stage subcontractors and is expected to generate thousands of jobs during both the design and construction phases.ATP Chief Executive Greg Canally said, "This is another major step forward, and we now have the full construction team that will deliver Austin’s light rail system. The Operations and Maintenance Facility is integral to the success of the system—it’s where trains are maintained, cleaned and prepared for service each day." Canally added that partnering with Kiewit, a world-class firm with deep local experience, positions the project for long-term success and broader economic opportunities across Central Texas. The LINC Austin program, initially led by Aecom, includes Parsons, STV, Turner & Townsend, Ardmore Roderick, and CAS Consulting among its delivery-partner collaborators.

+ AUSTIN TRANSIT PARTNERSHIP NAMES KAP TO LEAD OMF FOR PROJECT CONNECT

The Austin Transit Partnership's board has chosen Kiewit Austin Partnership, a joint venture of Kiewit Building Group Inc. and Austin Commercial, as the design-build contractor for the Operations and Maintenance Facility, enabling immediate pre-construction activities such as design advancement, permitting, and site preparation. The decision was announced on 21st April 2026.The OMF footprint covers about 62 acres on the southwest edge of the Montopolis neighborhood, near U.S. 183 and SH 71, and will house storage and servicing for up to 40 light rail vehicles. To make room for the facility, property acquisitions will affect dozens of existing businesses, with relocation concerns centered on Airport Commerce Park.KAP currently brings roughly 10 subcontractors into the design phase, with more work expected as construction ramps up. The broader Project Connect program is projected to create or support thousands—potentially tens of thousands—of jobs across Texas during design and construction. The OMF is expected to support about 300 long-term jobs once the system is operational, contributing to local economic growth. Mainline construction is expected to start in 2027, with initial service targeted for the early 2030s. The Federal Transit Administration published its final Environmental Impact Statement and Record of Decision in January 2026, allowing the project to move into final design and land acquisition.

+ KIEWIT TO LEAD PRE-FEED FOR ATERNIUM'S $1 BILLION HYDROGEN AND HEAVY WATER PLANT

Aternium has appointed Kiewit Engineering Group Inc. to undertake the pre-front-end engineering and design (pre-FEED) for its first integrated hydrogen and heavy water production facility, a step in its roughly $1 billion plan to produce clean hydrogen and critical heavy water domestically. The announcement was made on 26th March 2026.The Mid-Atlantic site is designed to meet rising demand for low-carbon energy and strengthen domestic capabilities for heavy water, which is currently sourced primarily from countries such as China and Iran. Andrew Cottone, founder and CEO of Aternium, noted that the collaboration with Kiewit aligns with the company's emphasis on technical excellence and safety standards.During the pre-FEED phase, Kiewit will help define the project scope, optimize design parameters, and lay a solid foundation for future development, according to the companies. Tyler Nordquist, executive vice president at Kiewit Engineering Group Inc., said the partnership will add substantial value in this early stage.Aternium's integrated production approach combines clean hydrogen generation with a patent-pending process for isolating heavy water, a dual output designed to lower hydrogen production costs and to address vulnerabilities in the U.S. supply of strategic materials. The project sits at the intersection of energy transition and advanced manufacturing and aims to deliver scalable benefits to local communities through workforce development and job creation.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ TURNER CELEBRATES TOPPING OUT OF 72-MW ALIGNED IAD-06 DATA CENTER IN FREDERICK

Turner Construction Company marked the topping out of the Aligned IAD-06 Data Center in Frederick, Maryland, signifying the completion of the structure for the 72-megawatt facility. The milestone was achieved on the 28th of April 2026, less than five months after ground had been broken. To date, the project has logged more than 80,000 craft hours, with roughly 345 skilled workers contributing to progress. The 450,000-square-foot center sits on a 75-acre campus and features a two-story structural steel design, an insulated precast exterior system, and a high-efficiency hybrid cooling solution that combines liquid and air-cooled technologies to optimize performance. Turner's leadership emphasized that topping out represents more than erecting the frame, underscoring the strength of the partnership with Aligned and the team’s commitment to safe, efficient delivery. As the project moves into interior fit-out and operational readiness, stakeholders will watch closely, anticipating the facility’s role in supporting the region’s digital infrastructure.

+ GEISINGER WYOMING VALLEY EXPANSION MOVES TO VERTICAL CONSTRUCTION AS TURNER BREAKS GROUND

WILKES-BARRE, PA — Turner Construction has advanced to vertical construction on Geisinger Wyoming Valley Medical Center's $900 million expansion, moving forward after years of planning and foundation work. Steel now rises on an 11-story, 600,000-square-foot patient tower that will lift beds from 356 to 500 and expand operating rooms from 12 to 18. Officials say the addition will enlarge the emergency department and ensure all patient rooms are private, with completion expected in the first quarter of 2028. The date of this announcement is 28th April 2026.David Kaminski, Turner’s Vice President and Senior General Manager, described the milestone as a key step toward broader health care access for families in northeastern Pennsylvania and a chance to partner with Geisinger on a project that strengthens the community for decades. The project is projected to create as many as 3,800 construction jobs and generate about $1 billion in local economic impact. Onsite employment is expected to rise from 150 workers to as many as 450 during peak activity, with Geisinger planning to hire an additional 400 staff after opening.Geisinger Wyoming Valley's campus opened in 1981 and has since expanded to meet rising demand for medical services. The new tower will bring advanced capabilities comparable to those typically found in major academic medical centers serving large metropolitan areas.

+ JMA VENTURES NAMES TURNER CONSTRUCTION LEAD ON WESTCOURT ORLANDO DEVELOPMENT

Turner Construction has been named the general contractor for the Westcourt Orlando mixed-use project, a development planned for West Church Street in downtown Orlando. The announcement was made by developers on the 22nd of April, 2026, marking a milestone in the project’s progression. The decision to partner with Turner stems from a collaborative approach taken by JMA Ventures. Todd Chapman, president of JMA Ventures, said that Turner’s team demonstrated exceptional capabilities in prior complex builds, including coordinating infrastructure for rocket launches on Florida’s Space Coast. He recalled an early meeting in which he told the team, "If you guys can send things into space, I’m pretty sure you can work with us." Turner has been involved in the Westcourt project since 2022, handling pre-construction with JMA Ventures and Machete Group. Early site activities such as utility mapping and surveying are underway to ensure a smooth transition into the next phases. The Westcourt project is valued at approximately $500 million and will include a hotel, residential units, office space, retail, and a live entertainment venue adjacent to the Kia Center, with peak on-site employment around 800 workers.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS [3 News That Matters]

+ AUDDIA AND THRAMANN CREATE MCCARTHY FINNEY, INC. IN STRATEGIC MERGER

Post-merger ownership is projected to be about 20% for Auddia and 80% for Thramann, subject to a minimum cash balance of $12 million at closing. The special preferred stock issued to Thramann carries a liquidation preference of at least $20.5 million and features flexible conversion and redemption terms, affording Thramann substantial governance influence over the merged entity.

+ CALIFORNIA TOWER TOPPING OUT SIGNALS PROGRESS IN UC DAVIS HEALTH MASTER PLAN

The California Tower is a 14-story hospital with a five-story pavilion designed to house operating rooms, an imaging center, and updated facilities for pharmacy and burn care, and will add about 334 inpatient beds. The tower is part of UC Davis Health's eight-year plan to double the campus size — the largest health-system capital expansion underway in the United States — with opening anticipated in 2030 as the project advances under Vision 2030. The initiative will expand campus space from roughly 3.6 million square feet to more than 7 million.

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS DETAILS 2024 EMISSIONS FOOTPRINT IN COMPREHENSIVE GHG REPORT

McCarthy Holdings, one of the largest employee-owned construction companies in the United States, released its updated 2024 GHG Emissions Report, published on 16th February 2026. Developed in partnership with Gravity, the report offers a thorough view of the company’s carbon footprint, covering Scope 1, Scope 2 and key Scope 3 emissions, with Scopes 1 and 2 undergoing independent auditing and limited assurance.The publication aligns with McCarthy’s mission to strengthen sustainability across its operations and subsidiaries and to uphold the standard of being the best builder in America. The company’s sustainability framework rests on three priorities: understanding environmental impact, acting responsibly for people and communities, and efficiently managing energy consumption throughout projects and operations.McCarthy also describes how emissions data informs broader responsible practices, including biodiversity initiatives and responsible fuel management. The organization leverages digital tools and artificial intelligence to improve project decisions and efficiency. A notable initiative is its PV recycling program for solar panels, conducted with SEIA-approved recyclers to divert waste from landfills and advance a circular economy.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP APPOINTS CARLY REINER CACERES AS NATIONAL CAMPUS RECRUITING HR DIRECTOR

Clark Construction Group appointed Carly Reiner Caceres as Director of Human Resources – National Campus Recruiting, effective Apr. 29, 2026, with a mandate to advance national campus recruiting initiatives and strengthen early-career talent pipelines for its broad portfolio of projects.Caceres arrives from Proper, where she served as Vice President of People Operations, shaping HR strategy and operations in a hybrid workplace. Her background also includes leadership roles at MILLER as Director of Internal Operations and more than nine years at Accenture as Talent Strategy Manager and Campus Recruiting Lead.Her prior experience at Grant Thornton LLP overseeing campus recruiting strategies further reinforces her capability to build robust internship programs and solid student engagement initiatives.Clark Construction Group, established in 1906, has a long track record across education and infrastructure markets. The company says Caceres will bring strategic HR expertise to attract top graduates and mentor the next generation of construction professionals.Her early career featured internships at Charleston Magazine and the Smithsonian Institution’s Hirshhorn Museum, rounding out a diverse background in communications and external relations that will help guide talent development efforts.

+ RENFREWSHIRE COUNCIL AWARDS MULTI-TRADES FRAMEWORK CONTRACT TO CLARK CONTRACTS FOR DOMESTIC HOUSING WORKS

Clark Contracts has been appointed to Renfrewshire Council's multi-trades contractors framework to improve delivery of repair, maintenance and refurbishment services for domestic properties across the authority. The appointment was announced on 11th February 2026 and the framework runs through 2027.The framework covers a broad range of multi-trade works, enabling the council to manage both responsive and planned maintenance more effectively across its housing stock and ensuring residents benefit from a faster, more reliable service.Clark Contracts is a well-established public-sector supplier, with current agreements including Scotland Excel’s New Build Residential Framework and NHS National Services Scotland’s Minor Works Framework. The company also participates in the Crown Commercial Service’s Construction Framework and several Scottish Procurement Alliance frameworks.Gordon Cunningham, managing director at Clark Contracts, said the appointment builds on a track record of more than £200 million in refurbishment projects over the past three years and demonstrates the firm’s commitment to serving local communities.The deal supports Clark Contracts’ growing public-sector footprint as it works with councils and institutions such as the University of Glasgow and Glasgow City Council to deliver essential housing services.

+ CDC APPROVES AMENDMENT FOR ₱4-B THE VILLAGES DEVELOPMENT, PKR JOINS CLARK GROWTH DRIVE

Clark Freeport is set to host a ₱4-billion mixed-use residential and commercial development in The Villages after Clark Development Corp. approved an amendment to PKR Corp.'s lease on December 13, 2025. The revised lease provides PKR with a 30-month window to complete construction and includes safeguards aimed at ensuring on-time delivery. Atty. Agnes VST Devanadera, CDC president and CEO, noted that the amendment reinforces investor confidence and the efficient execution of projects, while keeping The Villages as a community hub where residents can live and participate. PKR Corp. president Daekwon Jung thanked CDC for the partnership and said the company will deliver the Clark project, with PKR operating under Kyeryong Construction Industrial Co., Ltd., a South Korean firm known for safety, technology, and workplace practices. The initiative fits CDC's broader push to accelerate investments, promote well-planned communities, and establish a business-friendly environment that appeals to both global and local investors.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: Seminole Financial Services, LLC Finances Solar Project in Connecticut

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JKH-1BD3-RW07-P2KB-00000-00Seminole Financial Services, LLC Finances Solar Project in Connecticut2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:44:41ZLexisNexisSeminole Financial Services, LLC Finances Solar Project in Connecticut

Key Highlights:

* Seminole Financial Services closed a $2.1 million construction loan for a 0.96 MW solar project in Connecticut.

* The project marks Seminole's commitment to renewable energy finance, with over $3.5 billion invested since 2009.

* Seminole has financed over 400 solar and wind installations across the US and Hawaii, totaling 2.0+ GW in capacity.

Original Press Release:

Belleair Bluffs, Florida, May 8 -- Seminole Financial Services issued the following news release:

Seminole Financial Services ("SFS"), a leading provider of debt and tax credit equity financing for renewable energy transactions, announced the closing of a Solar transaction totaling $2.1 million construction loan. This solar project located in Connecticut has a capacity of 0.96 MW. Since 2009, Seminole has committed over $3.5 billion in construction and permanent debt financing plus tax credit equity for more than 400 solar and wind installations throughout the continental U.S. and Hawaii, for the equivalent of 2.0+ GW in installed capacity. For further details about Seminole Financial Services' renewable energy finance programs, please contact Chris Diaz, Principal, at (727) 460-0578.

About

Seminole Financial Services was founded with the goal of financing Renewable Energy and Real Estate projects. For over a decade, we've provided superior returns to our investors by creating innovative capital solutions for our development partners. Our vertically integrated team takes a collaborative approach to working with our stakeholders so that, together, we can build a more sustainable future. To date, we have directly funded over $3.5 Billion in capital for Renewable Energy and Multi-Family Real Estate projects nationwide.

Seminole's consistent returns and relationship-focus are grounded in the investment principles of its founder, Bob Banks. For over 30 years, Mr. Banks powered the success of the Midland Companies. During his tenure and title of Owner and CEO, Mr. Banks closed over 2,500 real estate investments and consistently generated above-market returns.

In 1999, Mr. Banks successfully sold the Midland Companies to a larger public entity and retired in 2003. Following his retirement, the funds' performance began to suffer and, in 2008 the pension funds asked Mr. Banks if he would consider exiting retirement to resume fund management utilizing his relationship-focused approach.

[Category: Renewable Energy]

Source: Seminole Financial Services

value.Title: SWINERTON INCORPORATED - SECTOR HIGHLIGHTS WITH PEER COMPARISONS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHM-RY53-RVFD-P0X0-00000-00SWINERTON INCORPORATED - SECTOR HIGHLIGHTS WITH PEER COMPARISONS2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:56ZLexisNexisSTOCKSWINERTON INCORPORATED - SECTOR HIGHLIGHTS WITH PEER COMPARISONS

DASHBOARD: SWINERTON INCORPORATED

PermID4296594671Websitehttp://www.swinerton.comIndustryConstruction & EngineeringAddress5707 SOUTHWEST PARKWAY, BLDG. 1, SUITE 200 | AUSTIN, TX 78735

ACTIVITIES:

Swinerton Incorporated provides commercial construction and construction management services in the Western United States. It offers general construction, modernization and renovation, management and consulting, preconstruction, corporate, and renewable energy services. The companys projects include aviation, cultural, education, federal, gaming and entertainment, healthcare, hospitality, interiors, mission critical, mixed-use/residential, office, renewable energy, retail, and special. Swinerton Incorporated was formerly known as Swinerton & Walberg Co. The company was founded in 1888 and is based in San Francisco, California with additional offices in California, Colorado, Hawaii, Texas, New Mexico, and Washington.

+ SWINERTON INCORPORATED [3 Press Releases]

+ SWINERTON TAPS PROVEN DUO TO DRIVE NATIONAL ACCOUNTS

With over $1 billion in growth and over 250 major projects under their belts, Swinerton's Chris Chany and Derek Jaschke step into new roles to drive client-focused expansion. Swinerton announces two strategic leadership promotions to continue delivering exceptional client experiences nationwide. Effective July 1, Chris Chany has been promoted to lead National Markets, while Derek Jaschke has been named Director of National Accounts, succeeding Chany.

Chany, known for his relationship-driven leadership style, previously served as Director of National Accounts, where he was instrumental in expanding Swinerton's client base and driving growth. Under his leadership, the national accounts sales model grew from $350 million to more than $1 billion in just four years. Chany has strengthened partnerships across multiple markets and sectors, generating repeat business.

"Chris' ability to strategize with teams from pre-sell to pursuit, through project execution and closeout, has allowed Swinerton to grow and solidify critical client relationships and seize growth opportunities for our teams," shares Don Adair, Swinerton's CRO. "Throughout his career, Chris has emphasized relationships, extraordinary client experiences, dependable project delivery and consistently generating repeat business."

Source: Company Website

+ SWINERTON INCORPORATED WILLIAM SILVA NAMED DIRECTOR OF NATIONAL MASS TIMBER

Instrumental leader to enhance Swinerton Builders' mass timber capabilities Swinerton, a national commercial general contractor and construction manager with office locations nationwide, continues its commitment to advancing and accelerating the adoption of mass timber construction with the appointment of William Silva as Director, National Mass Timber.

In this position, Silva will lead the creation of a Mass Timber Center of Excellence, a cross-functional initiative designed by Swinerton to drive innovation, collaboration and integrate the company's extensive general contracting expertise with its affiliate firm, Timberlab. Timberlab's specialized capabilities include mass timber procurement, manufacturing, fabrication, engineering and design. The center will serve as a hub for innovation, education, and operational excellence, empowering Swinerton teams nationwide to deliver exceptional mass timber projects and continue to be a trusted resource for its clients, design partners, and engineering partners.

Source: Company Website

+ SWINERTON TOPS-OUT LATEST MASS TIMBER MULTIFAMILY PROJECT IN CHARLOTTE

Swinerton, an industry-leading commercial general contractor with offices nationwide, announces the topping out of Cordo, an $80 million multifamily development in Charlotte's Optimist Park and the construction firm's fifth collaboration with Space Craft. Just blocks from The Joinery and Oxbow, also developed by Space Craft and built by Swinerton, Cordo is designed to be a walkable, transit-friendly community for active families and young professionals. The hybrid-structure broke ground last fall and is expected to be ready for occupancy by summer 2027.

"There is a distinct comfort level when you have a long-term relationship with your construction crew that spans multiple projects," says Brian Nicholson, director of construction, Space Craft. "Cordo is the fifth project we've embarked on with Swinerton, and the team continues to impress us with an unwavering commitment to quality and ability to deliver on our vision. With each community, we set out to build a sustainable, modern neighborhood for today. Swinerton has been instrumental in helping us shape the Mill District into one of Charlotte's hottest neighborhoods."

Situated on a three-acre site in Charlotte's Optimist Park community, Cordo totals seven floors with 3,000 square feet of street-level retail just steps from Cordelia Park, Optimist Hall and LYNX Blue Line light rail access. The 325,000-square foot development features 287studio, one-, two-, three- and four-bedroom units and offers premium amenities including a rooftop terrace, private courtyard, electric bicycles for rent, a fitness center, a shared dining area and coworking and meeting space. A Mass timber brings a biophilic element into each residence with cross-laminated timber (CLT) decks creating exposed timber ceilings. Charlotte architecture firm Shook Kelley designed the community located at 515 Parkwood Ave.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ AUSTIN INDUSTRIES, INC. [3 Press Releases]

+ AUSTIN NAMED NO. 1 SCIENCE + TECHNOLOGY CONSTRUCTION FIRM

DALLAS - Austin is honored to once again be named the No. 1 Science + Technology Facility Construction Firm in the U.S. by World of Construction.

‍

From semiconductor fabs to digital infrastructure, our teams pour their skill, care, and dedication into creating the specialized spaces that help fuel tomorrow's breakthroughs. It's thanks to our employee‑owners, partners, and clients that this work is possible, and we're grateful for the trust they place in us every day.

‍

Firms recognized by World of Construction are those that are leading the way in delivering some of the most advanced science and technology facilities in the world. Austin is grateful for the recognition and proud to be leading our industry.

Source: Company Website

+ AUSTIN EARNS MULTIPLE HONORS AT ASA HOUSTON EVENT

HOUSTON - Austin Commercial was honored to be finalists for multiple Excellence in Construction Awards from American Subcontractors Association-Houston Chapter. Austin's Houston office was awarded the GC Safety Award and the Project of the Year Over $50M for the UH Memorial Hermann Football Operations Center.

‍

The company extends its thanks to our trade partners and employee-owners for all that you do, and for making these honors possible.

Source: Company Website

+ AUSTIN RANKED #27 ON BD+C'S TOP 100 HOSPITAL FACILITY CONSTRUCTION FIRMS

DALLAS - Austin has been named No. 27 on Building Design + Construction's (BD+C) 2025 Top 100 Hospital Facility Construction Firms list, reaffirming the company's long-standing position as a trusted builder in the healthcare market, and highlighting continued growth in this sector.

Austin has appeared on BD+C's healthcare rankings for years, but recent performance reflects a notable expansion in both volume and opportunity. That momentum is already visible in the work underway. In 2025, Austin began preconstruction on several major healthcare projects, including JPS Health Network Hospital Addition, The University of Texas at Austin & The University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center Medical Complex Project (MCP), and The University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center Patient Care Building 1 (1PC).

This ranking is a testament to the expertise and dedication of Austin's employee-owners and highlights the importance of continued investment in people and resources as healthcare remains a key growth sector for the company.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ QUANTA SERVICES, INC. [1 Press Release]

+ QUANTA SERVICES TO HOST & WEBCAST ITS 2026 INVESTOR DAY

Quanta Services, Inc. (NYSE: PWR) announced that it will host and webcast its 2026 Investor Day from New York City on Tues., Mar. 31, 2026. Management intends to present certain information and engage in a question-and-answer discussion focused on the company's strategic initiatives and new long-term financial targets. The management presentation portion of the event is scheduled to begin at approximately 9:00 a.m. Eastern time and the entire event, including the question-and-answer portion, is expected to conclude by approximately 11:30 a.m. Eastern time.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BARTON MALOW COMPANY [4 Press Releases]

+ BARTON MALOW + SHERIDAN CONSTRUCTION SET TO BUILD NEW MACON ARENA

MACON, GEORGIA - Barton Malow, in association with Sheridan Construction, has been chosen as the Construction Manager for the new Macon Arena, a major project that will replace the current Macon Coliseum and serve as a key part of the region's expanding entertainment opportunities.

Located southeast of Atlanta in Macon-Bibb County, the planned facility is designed to host various multipurpose events like minor league hockey, basketball games, high school tournaments, concerts, conferences, trade shows, and more. The new arena will offer a top-tier guest experience, featuring premium seating options like luxury suites, club suites, and loge boxes, as well as modern fan amenities such as clubs, concessions, restrooms, locker rooms, and integrated technologies. Back-of-house and support spaces will ensure the venue functions as a state-of-the-art civic facility, offering performers, event planners, and organizations a great experience.

"This arena won't be just a venue, it will be a destination and major economic engine that creates opportunities, attracts visitors, and keeps our city competitive for years to come," said Macon-Bibb Mayor Lester Miller.

As Macon's entertainment landscape continues to expand with the new Atrium Health Amphitheater and the recently renovated Macon City Auditorium, the new arena is poised to further elevate the region's growing lineup of elite venues.

"We're thrilled to partner with Sheridan Construction and bring Barton Malow's expertise to Macon-Bibb to deliver a top-tier arena. The new venue is set to become a central location in Georgia for sports, entertainment, and community gatherings," said Barton Malow Office Director Blake Boyd. "This project is an investment in Macon-Bibb County's future, and we're excited to support the local community every step of the way."

Adds Sheridan Construction President + CEO Christy Kovac. "It's a privilege for our firm to return to Macon's legacy of entertainment and civic pride, having built the original coliseum 57 years ago. Partnering with Barton Malow on this transformative new arena is both an honor and a responsibility. We believe this project will serve as a powerful economic and tourism engine, attracting visitors, creating opportunities, and enriching the lives of everyone in our community for generations to come."

Construction is planned to commence in summer 2026, with the project anticipated to reach completion in 2028.

Source: Company Website

+ BARTON MALOW COMPANY LUKE KNOCHEL PROMOTED TO VICE PRESIDENT OF PROJECT CONTROLS

Luke's project controls leadership role spans multiple geographic regions and consists of overseeing directors and managers across Barton Malow's operations.

Barton Malow has announced the promotion of Luke Knochel to Vice President of Project Controls.

In his new role, Luke leads a multi‑disciplinary team responsible for core functions such as cost and schedule management, forecasting, project start‑up, accounts receivable and payable processes, and change management. His leadership spans multiple geographic regions, overseeing directors and managers across Barton Malow's operations.

Luke started at Barton Malow in 2013 as a Cost Analyst and has served in various project controls and finance leadership roles throughout his tenure. He oversaw accounts payable and accounts receivable, change management, forecasting, reporting, and more on the Little Caesars Arena project, then went on to supervise Barton Malow's offices in Chicago, Columbus, and Nashville. After a promotion to Project Controls Manager overseeing the then-Midwest business unit, Luke transitioned into a corporate finance role managing the shared service department and implementing new accounts payable software.

NIL athletic construction: Many modern facilities now include dedicated content creation zones where athletes can produce high-quality photos and videos for social media and sponsorship campaigns.

On July 1, 2021, the NCAA formally adopted a policy allowing college athletes to earn money for their name, image, and likeness (NIL).

At the time, one of my acquaintances, Matt Wilson, was serving as the Senior Director of Development for Athletics at Embry-Riddle Aeronautical University. He witnessed firsthand the immediate impact that NIL had on college athletics.

Today, Matt sees things from the other side of the table. After leaving Embry-Riddle in 2022, he spent two years as Executive Director of NIL Collective Operations + Development at Athlete Forward Initiative. Now he runs his own consulting group designed to provide leadership, fundraising, communication, and culture development training for college athletic departments.

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION ENTERPRISES, INC. [2 Press Releases]

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION BREAKS GROUND ON $190 MILLION CONTRACT FOR STATE-OF-THE-ART DRINKING WATER FACILITY

DENVER, Colo. (Sept. 25, 2025) - PCL Construction, Inc. celebrated the groundbreaking of a $190 million contract to build the Westminster Boulevard Drinking Water Facility - an innovative project that employs advanced treatment technology to ensure clean water to residents.

The City of Westminster entrusted the PCL team to build the project's most critical components. As the lead contractor, PCL will manage the construction of cutting-edge filtration and treatment technologies. The facility will feature advanced oxidation and filtration systems designed to improve taste and odor, while enhancing resiliency against fluctuations in incoming water quality.

Designed with long-term sustainability in mind, the project replaces aging infrastructure and will provide reliable drinking water to nearly 33,000 homes and businesses. Once operational the facility will have the capacity to treat 14.7 million gallons of water per day (MGD).

"PCL is proud to celebrate the opening of the Westminster Boulevard Drinking Water Facility with the City of Westminster. This is an essential project that secures safe, reliable drinking water for the community," said Ankur Talwar, PCL's West region district manager.

The groundbreaking ceremony brought together Westminster City Council members, build partners, local leaders, and community members to celebrate the future site.

The facility is slated to be finished in 2028.

Source: Company Website

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION AND PHOENIX CHILDREN'S: BUILDING A STRONGER FUTURE FOR PEDIATRIC CARE

PHOENIX, Ariz. (November 10, 2025) - PCL Construction, Inc. and Phoenix Children's are proud to celebrate a partnership dedicated to advancing pediatric health and supporting children facing critical medical challenges in Arizona.

For eight consecutive years, PCL Construction in Arizona has deepened its commitment to Phoenix Children's, increasing its annual donation to $50,000 in support of pediatric cancer care. This ongoing collaboration directly benefits children by funding medical specialists, cutting-edge technology, and family-centered care.

"Our partnership with Phoenix Children's is about more than sponsorship-it's about building hope and supporting families during their most difficult times," said Josh Yates, construction risk manager at PCL Construction. "We are honored to contribute to the hospital's mission and to help create brighter futures for children in our community."

As part of this partnership, PCL proudly sponsors Phoenix Children's annual Big Dig for Kids event. This unique experience gives pediatric patients a chance to explore construction equipment, participate in hands-on activities, and enjoy a day filled with fun and inspiration. The event not only raises vital funds for pediatric cancer care but also brings moments of joy to children and families, helping them envision a future beyond illness.
